item.title: 【2026 Developer Research Report】2026 年开发者研究报告
item.description:
【

Hello, our dearest DZone Community!

Last year, we asked you for your thoughts on emerging and evolving software development trends, your day-to-day as devs, and workflows that work best — all to shape our 2026 Community Research Report. The goal is simple: to better understand our community and provide the right content and resources developers need to support their career journeys.

】

您好，我们最亲爱的 DZone 社区！

去年，我们询问了您对新兴和不断发展的软件开发趋势、您作为开发人员的日常工作以及最有效的工作流程的看法 - 所有这些都是为了塑造我们的 2026 年社区研究报告。目标很简单：更好地了解我们的社区并提供开发人员支持其职业生涯所需的正确内容和资源。

item.title: 【Modern State Management: Signals, Observables, and Server Components】现代状态管理：信号、可观察量和服务器组件
item.description:
【

State management is a critical aspect of modern web applications. In the Angular ecosystem, reactivity has long been powered by observables (RxJS), a powerful but sometimes complex paradigm. Angular’s recent introduction of signals provides a new, intuitive reactivity model to simplify UI state handling. Meanwhile, frameworks like React are exploring server components that push some state management to the server side. 

This article compares these approaches: observables, signals, and server components, and when to use each in modern development.

】

状态管理是现代 Web 应用程序的一个重要方面。在 Angular 生态系统中，反应性长期以来一直由 observables (RxJS) 提供支持，这是一种强大但有时很复杂的范例。 Angular 最近引入的s信号提供了一种新的、直观的反应模型来简化 UI 状态处理。与此同时，像 React 这样的框架正在探索将一些状态管理推送到服务器端的s服务器组件。 

本文比较了这些方法：可观察量、信号和服务器组件，以及何时在现代开发中使用每种方法。

item.title: 【Software Testing in LLMs: The Shift Towards Autonomous Testing】法学硕士中的软件测试：向自主测试的转变
item.description:
【

I wanted to unpack a simple, clear reality on intelligent testing in the large language models (LLM) era. LLMs redefine software testing principles by accelerating intelligent testing across the entire SDLC, enabling autonomous test generation, self-verifying AI agents, and true shift-left quality across build and deployment pipelines.

Why Are LLMs a Testing Game-Changer?

The "why" cuts to the heart of testing's oldest challenges: People write tests. People maintain flaky scripts. People explore complex systems. These tasks are deeply rooted in language (specifications, bug reports, code) and reasoning (what to test next, why something failed). LLMs have learned the patterns of code, natural language, and logical discourse from a vast corpus of human knowledge. They can now participate in the intellectual work of testing.

】

我想揭示大语言模型 (LLM) 时代智能测试的简单、清晰的现实。法学硕士通过加速整个 SDLC 的智能测试、实现自主测试生成、自我验证 AI 代理以及跨构建和部署管道的真正左移质量，重新定义了软件测试原则。

为什么法学硕士是测试游戏规则的改变者？

“为什么”切入了测试最古老的挑战的核心：人们编写测试。人们维护脆弱的脚本。人们探索复杂的系统。这些任务深深植根于语言（规范、错误报告、代码）和推理（接下来要测试什么、为什么失败）。法学硕士从庞大的人类知识库中学习了代码模式、自然语言和逻辑话语。他们现在可以参与测试的智力工作。

item.title: 【Hands-On With Kubernetes 1.35】实践 Kubernetes 1.35
item.description:
【

Kubernetes 1.35 was released on December 17, 2025, bringing significant improvements for production workloads, particularly in resource management, AI/ML scheduling, and authentication. Rather than just reading the release notes, I decided to test these features hands-on in a real Azure VM environment.

This article documents my journey testing four key features in Kubernetes 1.35:

】

Kubernetes 1.35 于 2025 年 12 月 17 日发布，为生产工作负载带来了显着改进，特别是在资源管理、AI/ML 调度和身份验证方面。我决定在真实的 Azure VM 环境中亲自测试这些功能，而不是仅仅阅读发行说明。

本文记录了我测试 Kubernetes 1.35 中四个关键功能的历程：

item.title: 【Failure Handling in AI Pipelines: Designing Retries Without Creating Chaos】人工智能管道中的故障处理：设计重试而不造成混乱
item.description:
【

Retries have become an integral part of the AI tools or systems. In most systems I have seen, teams usually approach failures with blanket retrying. This often yields duplicate work, cost spikes, wasted compute, and operational instability. Every unnecessary retry triggers another inference call, an embedding request, or a downstream write, without improving the outcome.

In most early-stage AI tools, the pattern is that if a request fails, a retry is added. If the retry succeeds intermittently, then the logic is considered sufficient. This approach works fine until the application is in the test environment or in low-user-usage mode; as soon as the application sees higher traffic and concurrent execution, retries begin to dominate system behavior.

】

重试已成为人工智能工具或系统不可或缺的一部分。在我见过的大多数系统中，团队通常通过一揽子重试来解决失败问题。这通常会导致重复工作、成本飙升、计算浪费和运营不稳定。每次不必要的重试都会触发另一个推理调用、嵌入请求或下游写入，而不会改善结果。

在大多数早期阶段的 AI 工具中，其模式是，如果请求失败，则会添加重试。如果重试间歇性地成功，那么逻辑就被认为是足够的。在应用程序处于测试环境或低用户使用模式之前，这种方法可以正常工作；一旦应用程序看到更高的流量和并发执行，重试就开始主导系统行为。

item.title: 【Reducing Daily PM Overhead With a Chat-Based AI Agent】使用基于聊天的 AI 代理减少每日 PM 开销
item.description:
【

As a project manager, I have often encountered time losses caused by daily operational routines. Depending on how many departments are involved in development, these delays can range from two extra days per task to one or even two weeks for a relatively small feature.

These delays usually occur in processes not directly related to development itself: clarifying requirements, working in task trackers, searching for information, duplicating work, and constantly switching between tasks. This is also supported by research: around 90% of professionals say they regularly lose time because of inefficient processes and tools, and about half of them lose more than 10 hours every week because of this.

】

作为一名项目经理，我经常遇到因日常运营而造成的时间损失。根据参与开发的部门数量，这些延迟的范围可能从每个任务额外两天到相对较小的功能的一周甚至两周不等。

这些延迟通常发生在与开发本身不直接相关的流程中：澄清需求、在任务跟踪器中工作、搜索信息、重复工作以及不断在任务之间切换。这也得到了研究的支持：大约 90% 的专业人士表示，他们经常因为流程和工具效率低下而浪费时间，其中大约一半的人因此每周损失超过 10 个小时。

item.title: 【When Million Requests Arrive in a Minute: Why Reactive Auto Scaling Fails and the Predictive Fix】当一分钟内到达百万个请求时：为什么响应式自动扩展会失败以及预测性修复
item.description:
【

Reactive autoscaling is a critical safety net. Demand rises, metrics spike, policies trigger, and capacity increases. But flash-crowd events, product drops, major campaigns, and limited-inventory moments do not ramp. They cliff. Users arrive at once, and reactive scaling is structurally late because “scale triggered” is only the start of the journey to usable capacity.

If your demand spike arrives faster than your system can warm up, reactive scaling will lag no matter how well you tune it. The fix is planning and verification, scaling before the event, and proving the system is ready before customers arrive.

】

反应式自动缩放是一个重要的安全网。需求上升、指标激增、政策触发和容量增加。但闪购活动、产品下降、重大活动和库存有限的时刻不会增加。他们悬崖勒马。用户立即到达，而反应式扩展在结构上是较晚的，因为“扩展触发”只是可用容量之旅的开始。

如果您的需求峰值到达的速度比系统预热的速度快，则无论您调整得如何好，反应式扩展都会滞后。解决方法是在活动之前进行规划和验证、扩展，并在客户到达之前证明系统已准备就绪。

item.title: 【Fabric's Resource Governance and Scaling Pitfalls】Fabric 的资源治理和扩展陷阱
item.description:
【

Performance and Operational Pitfalls When Scaling BI on Fabric

Microsoft chose cost predictability over elasticity for the Fabric billing model. While Fabric’s capacity model simplifies setup, there is a high chance of depleting shared compute resources of a capacity, as well as paying for more resources than necessary. 

Common Pitfalls 

Fabric capacity scaling is manual, and no auto-scaling is available at present. While this provides absolute control over cost, the whole capacity planning burden is on the admin.

】

在 Fabric 上扩展 BI 时的性能和操作陷阱

对于 Fabric 计费模型，Microsoft 选择了成本可预测性而不是弹性。虽然 Fabric 的容量模型简化了设置，但很有可能会耗尽容量的共享计算资源，并且需要支付超出必要数量的资源。 

常见陷阱

Fabric 容量扩展是手动的，目前不支持自动扩展。虽然这提供了对成本的绝对控制，但整个容量规划的负担都由管理员承担。

item.title: 【The A3 Handoff Canvas】A3 交接画布
item.description:
【

TL; DR: The A3 Handoff Canvas

The A3 Framework helps you decide whether AI should touch a task (Assist, Automate, Avoid). The A3 Handoff Canvas covers what teams often skip: how to run the handoff without losing quality or accountability. It is a six-part workflow contract for recurring AI use: task splitting, inputs, outputs, validation, failure response, and record-keeping. If you cannot write one part down, that is where errors and excuses will enter.

The Handoff Canvas closes a gap in a useful pattern: from an unstructured prompt to applying the A3 Framework to document decisions with the A3 Handoff Canvas, to creating transferable skills, potentially leading to building agents.

】

TL； DR：A3 切换画布

A3 框架可帮助您决定 AI 是否应该接触某项任务（协助、自动化、避免）。 A3 交接画布涵盖了团队经常忽略的内容：如何在不损失质量或责任的情况下进行交接。它是一个由六部分组成的工作流程合同，用于重复使用人工智能：任务分割、输入、输出、验证、故障响应和记录保存。如果你不能写下某一部分，那就会出现错误和借口。

Handoff Canvas 缩小了有用模式中的差距：从非结构化提示到应用 A3 框架以使用 A3 Handoff Canvas 记录决策，再到创建可转移技能，可能会导致构建代理。

item.title: 【Deterministic AI With OpenSymbolicAI】使用 OpenSymbolicAI 的确定性 AI
item.description:
【

While AI agents have shifted programming away from deterministic algorithms toward probabilistic LLMs, there remains concern that the lack of determinism makes an agentic solution inherently unreliable. The question comes down to this: Is non-determinism acceptable?

The answer depends on what the solution is for. For creative endeavours such as ideation or writing fiction, non-deterministic responses can be a strength. But I'm sure we can agree that software that relies on precise results, such as those used in finance or scientific research, cannot accept non-determinism.

】

虽然人工智能代理已将编程从确定性算法转向概率性法学硕士，但仍然有人担心，缺乏确定性会使代理解决方案本质上不可靠。问题归结为：非决定论可以接受吗？

答案取决于解决方案的用途。对于构思或写小说等创造性活动来说，非确定性反应可能是一种优势。但我确信我们可以同意，依赖精确结果的软件（例如金融或科学研究中使用的软件）不能接受非确定性。
